zh.lifeSummary: Pierre Idosad Artaud of Aix Lord of Aix en diois是Guillaume Artaud d'Aix en Diois的兒子，於1185年出生在法國o-阿爾培斯德o姆艾--迪瓦斯。他於大約1230年與Saure de Mévouillon結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1263年11月11日享年78歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Pierre Idosad Artaud of Aix Lord of Aix en diois是Guillaume Artaud d'Aix en Diois的儿子，于1185年出生在法国o-阿爾培斯德o姆艾--迪瓦斯。他于大约1230年与Saure de Mévouillon结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1263年11月11日享年78岁。

nameMeanings.name: Artaud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Michel, Antoine, Jacques, Altagrace, Anatole, Andre, Angelle, Christophe, Lucien, Philippe.

French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements arn ‘eagle’ + wald ‘rule, power’ (see Arnold ). This surname is also found in the Dominican Republic and Haiti. Compare Renaud and Reneau .
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Arnoux
meanings: Altered form of French Arnould, a variant of Arnaud .
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Arnwulf, composed of the elements arn ‘eagle’ + wulf ‘wolf’.

variants.name: Arnoult
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Patrice, Pierre.
French: variant of   Arnaud  .

variants.name: Arnold
meanings: English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Hungarian, Czech, Slovak, Polish, Croatian, and Slovenian: from the ancient Germanic personal name Arnwald (Middle English Arnold, Old French Arnaut), composed of the elements arn ‘eagle’ + wald ‘rule, power’. This name was introduced to Britain by the Normans.
English: habitational name from either of two places called Arnold in Nottinghamshire and East Yorkshire, from Old English earn ‘eagle’ + halh ‘nook’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of the German personal name (see 1 above), at least in part on account of its resemblance to the Jewish name   Aaron  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Aix
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Normand, Emile, Francoise, Gilles, Jean-Guy, Laurier, Marcel.
French: from the Old French female personal name Alix or Alis, a short form of the ancient Germanic personal name Adalhaidis (see Allis ). See also Alie .

variants.name: Alie
meanings: French: variant of   Alix  . See also   Allie   and   Ally  .
Muslim (Indonesia): variant of   Ali  .

History: It was Pierre Alie (also Allie), born c. 1720 in Normandy, France, who first brought this surname to North America (QC). His father's surname is documented as both Alie and Alix, while some of Pierre's descendants also bear the surname Ally.

variants.name: Allis
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Alis, Alice (Old French Adeliz, Ahelis, Aalis, Aliz). Alice, together with its pet form Alison, was one of the most popular names in both England and Scotland from the 13th century until the end of the medieval period. The personal name is of ancient Germanic origin, brought to England from France by the Normans; it is a shortened form of ancient Germanic Adalheidis, which is composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + haid ‘‘rank, worth’’.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Aix
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Normand, Emile, Francoise, Gilles, Jean-Guy, Laurier, Marcel.
French: from the Old French female personal name Alix or Alis, a short form of the ancient Germanic personal name Adalhaidis (see Allis ). See also Alie .

variants.name: Alie
meanings: French: variant of   Alix  . See also   Allie   and   Ally  .
Muslim (Indonesia): variant of   Ali  .

History: It was Pierre Alie (also Allie), born c. 1720 in Normandy, France, who first brought this surname to North America (QC). His father's surname is documented as both Alie and Alix, while some of Pierre's descendants also bear the surname Ally.

variants.name: Allis
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Alis, Alice (Old French Adeliz, Ahelis, Aalis, Aliz). Alice, together with its pet form Alison, was one of the most popular names in both England and Scotland from the 13th century until the end of the medieval period. The personal name is of ancient Germanic origin, brought to England from France by the Normans; it is a shortened form of ancient Germanic Adalheidis, which is composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + haid ‘‘rank, worth’’.

nameMeanings.name: en
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 鄧, see   Deng   1.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 丁, see   Ding   1.
Chinese: possibly also from Taishanese form of the Chinese name 進 (meaning ‘progress’), a monosyllabic personal name or part of a disyllabic personal name of some early Chinese immigrants in the US.

variants.name: Deng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Hua, Jian, Jing, Wei, Wen, Yi, Ying, Chao, Fang, Gang, Hui, Min.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鄧: (i) from Deng (鄧), the name of a state (located in Dengxian in Henan province) granted to Man Ji, uncle of Wu Ding (a king of the Shang dynasty (1600 –1046 BC )). (ii) from Deng Bowen (鄧伯溫), head of an ancient state (located in present-day Shandong province) dating back to the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (iii) from Deng (鄧), a fief (located in Hubei province) granted to descendants of Zhong Kang, the 4th king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600 BC ). (iv) from Deng Cheng (鄧城, City of Deng), a town in the state of Cai (located in present-day Henan province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). (v) from Deng (鄧), a title of Li Tianyi, the 8th son of Li Yu (the last king of the state of Southern Tang (937–975 AD ) during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms (907–960) periods). The state of Southern Tang was annexed by Emperor Taizong of Song (939–997 AD ) in 975 AD , and the royal families of South Tang were hunted down. Li Tianyi's son fled and changed the surname (Li) to Deng (鄧), his father's title.

nameMeanings.name: diois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Portuguese and Galician: from Dinis, a Portuguese personal name equivalent to Dennis .

variants.name: Dennis
meanings: English: from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Denis(e), Dinis(e), vernacular forms of Latin Dionysius, Greek Dionysios ‘(follower) of Dionysos’, an eastern god introduced to the classical pantheon at a relatively late date and bearing a name of probably Semitic origin. The name was borne by various early Christian saints, including Saint Denis, the martyred 3rd-century bishop of Paris who became the patron saint of France. From the 12th century onward, the popularity of the name in England (and of its cognates, mainly Denis , in the continental Europe) seems to have been largely due to French influence. The feminine form Dionysia (in vernacular English likewise Den(n)is) is also found, and some examples of the surname may originate from it.
English: from Middle English deneis, a variant of danais ‘Danish’, with the vowel influenced by Middle English denshe (Old English denisc). Compare Dence .

Irish (mainly Dublin and Cork): of the same origin as 1 and 2 above, sometimes an alternative form to Donohue but more often to McDonough , since the personal name Donnchadh was Anglicized as Donough or Denis.

nameMeanings.name: Pierre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From New Testament Greek. See Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: Pierre Idosad Artaud of Aix Lord of Aix en diois was born in 1185, in Aix-en-Diois, Drôme, Rhône-Alpes, France as the son of Guillaume Artaud d'Aix en Diois. He married Saure de Mévouillon about 1230. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 11 November 1263, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Pierre Idosad Artaud of Aix Lord of Aix en diois est né en 1185, à Aix-en-Diois, Drôme, Rhône-Alpes, France, fils de Guillaume Artaud d'Aix en Diois. Il a épousé Saure de Mévouillon environ 1230. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 11 novembre 1263, à l’âge de 78 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Pierre Idosad Artaud of Aix Lord of Aix en diois wurde 1185 in Aix-en-Diois, Drôme, Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich, als Sohn von Guillaume Artaud d'Aix en Diois geboren. Er hat um 1230 Saure de Mévouillon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 11. November 1263 im Alter von 78 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Pierre Idosad Artaud of Aix Lord of Aix en diois è nato il nel 1185 nel Aix-en-Diois, Drôme, Rhône-Alpes, Francia come figlio di Guillaume Artaud d'Aix en Diois. Lui sposato Saure de Mévouillon circa 1230. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 11 novembre 1263, all'età di 78 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Pierre Idosad Artaud of Aix Lord of Aix en dioisは1185年にフランスルhoネ-アルペスドルoメエイクス-エン-ディオイスでGuillaume Artaud d'Aix en Dioisの息子として生まれた。約1230年にSaure de Mévouillonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1263年11月11日に78歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Pierre Idosad Artaud of Aix Lord of Aix en diois(은)는 1185년 프랑스 ho니-알프스 드o 아이스--디오이스에서 Guillaume Artaud d'Aix en Diois의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1230년 Saure de Mévouillon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1263년 11월 11일 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Pierre Idosad Artaud of Aix Lord of Aix en diois nasceu em 1185, em Aix-en-Diois, Drôme, Rhône-Alpes, França filho de Guillaume Artaud d'Aix en Diois. Ele casou-se com Saure de Mévouillon aproximadamente 1230. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 11 de novembro de 1263, com 78 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Pierre Idosad Artaud of Aix Lord of Aix en diois родился в 1185 г. в местности эикс-ен-диоис, дрoм, рoне-алпесь, Франция. Его отца звали Guillaume Artaud d'Aix en Diois. Он женился на женщине по имени Saure de Mévouillon около 1230 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 11 ноября 1263 г. в возрасте 78 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Pierre Idosad Artaud of Aix Lord of Aix en diois nació en 1185, en Aix-en-Diois, Drôme, Rhône-Alpes, Francia como hijo de Guillaume Artaud d'Aix en Diois. Se casó con Saure de Mévouillon aproximadamente 1230. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 11 de noviembre de 1263, a la edad de 78 años.
